title: Santa Trinita Maesta
gloss: Cimabue's monumental altarpiece enthrones the Virgin among angels and prophets on a towering gold ground. Its Byzantine formality, softened by hints of real depth, made it a bridge to the naturalism of his pupil Giotto.

title: Annunciation with St. Margaret and St. Ansanus
wikipedia: Annunciation with St. Margaret and St. Ansanus
gloss: Simone Martini's gold-ground altarpiece for Siena Cathedral catches the Virgin recoiling from Gabriel's embossed greeting. Its swaying line and courtly elegance epitomize Sienese Gothic painting.

title: Ognissanti Madonna
gloss: Giotto gives the enthroned Virgin real weight and a believable space, breaking with flat Byzantine convention. Painted for Florence's Ognissanti church, it marks a turning point toward naturalism in Western painting.

title: Portrait of a Man in a Red Turban
wikipedia: Portrait of a Man in a Red Turban
gloss: Van Eyck's man in a flamboyant red chaperon meets the viewer's gaze directly, most likely the painter himself. The frame's motto 'Als Ich Can' and minute oil detail make it a touchstone of early Netherlandish portraiture.

title: The Battle of San Romano
gloss: Uccello turns a Florentine cavalry clash into a pageant of perspective, with broken lances laid along the orthogonals. Its rocking-horse steeds and bold geometry show the early Renaissance obsession with constructed space.

title: Lamentation over the Dead Christ
gloss: Mantegna foreshortens the dead Christ feet-first toward the viewer, compressing the body into a stark sculptural slab. Its radical perspective and gray pallor make it one of the most arresting images of the Renaissance.

title: Self-Portrait at Twenty-Eight
gloss: Durer faces the viewer frontally like an icon of Christ, fur-trimmed and long-haired, asserting the artist's God-given creative power. It is the boldest of his self-portraits and a landmark of Northern Renaissance self-fashioning.

title: Young Hare
gloss: Durer's watercolor study renders a wild hare with astonishing fidelity, its fur built from thousands of layered strokes. A touchstone of Northern Renaissance naturalism and one of the most reproduced nature studies in art.

title: Portrait of Doge Leonardo Loredan
gloss: Bellini portrays Venice's doge in brocaded robes of state with the calm permanence of a marble bust, modeled in clear Venetian light. It set the standard for official portraiture in oil.

title: Portrait of Henry VIII
gloss: Holbein fixed the defining image of Henry VIII: broad-shouldered, frontal, and magnificently dressed. Copied across Tudor England after the original mural burned, it turned royal portraiture into an instrument of power.
imageFile: Hans Holbein, the Younger, Around 1497-1543 - Portrait of Henry VIII of England - Google Art Project.jpg

title: Madonna with the Long Neck
gloss: Parmigianino elongates the Virgin's neck and fingers into elegant artifice, with a single unfinished column behind. Its refined distortion of natural proportion is the textbook example of Mannerist style.

title: Vertumnus
gloss: Arcimboldo portrays Emperor Rudolf II as the Roman god of the seasons, his face composed entirely of fruits, flowers, and vegetables. The witty composite head is the most famous of his painted puzzles.

title: View of Toledo
gloss: El Greco rearranges Toledo beneath a churning green-black storm sky, trading topographic truth for spiritual drama. It stands among the first great expressive landscapes in Western art.

title: Conversion on the Way to Damascus
wikipedia: Conversion on the Way to Damascus
gloss: Caravaggio throws Saul flat beneath his horse, arms flung open to a blinding light only he perceives. The radical close-up and deep tenebrism made this Cerasi Chapel canvas a manifesto of Baroque religious drama.

title: David with the Head of Goliath
wikipedia: David with the Head of Goliath (Caravaggio, Rome)
David with the Head of Goliath (Caravaggio)
gloss: Caravaggio's somber David holds out Goliath's severed head, which bears the fugitive painter's own features. Made near the end of his life, it reads as both a plea for pardon and a stark meditation on guilt.

title: The Storm on the Sea of Galilee
wikipedia: The Storm on the Sea of Galilee
gloss: Rembrandt's only seascape pitches Christ and the panicking disciples on a cresting wave of light. Stolen from Boston's Gardner Museum in 1990, it remains the most famous missing painting in the world.

title: Belshazzar's Feast
gloss: Rembrandt freezes the instant divine writing blazes across the wall as Belshazzar twists in terror and wine spills from golden vessels. A bravura Baroque display of light, brocade, and theatrical shock.

title: Magdalene with the Smoking Flame
gloss: La Tour's Magdalene meditates by a single oil lamp, a skull on her lap, in near-total darkness. His serene candlelit geometry gives Baroque tenebrism a stillness closer to silence than drama.

title: Portrait of Innocent X
gloss: Velazquez caught the pope so truthfully that Innocent X reportedly pronounced it 'too real.' Its crimson intensity later obsessed Francis Bacon, who painted dozens of screaming variations.

title: The Goldfinch
gloss: Fabritius's small trompe-l'oeil panel shows a chained goldfinch on its feeder in plain daylight. Painted the year he died in the Delft gunpowder explosion, it links Rembrandt's studio to Vermeer's quiet luminosity.

title: Pierrot (Gilles)
gloss: Watteau's life-size sad clown stands isolated in white satin while fellow comedians mill below. Its melancholy beneath the theater costume made it an enduring emblem of the artist as outsider.

title: A Young Girl Reading
gloss: Fragonard's lemon-yellow profile of a girl absorbed in her little book is painted with dashing, creamy speed. A beloved Rococo image of private pleasure and effortless brushwork.

title: Self-Portrait in a Straw Hat
gloss: Vigee Le Brun presents herself fresh-faced and confident, palette in hand, in open homage to Rubens. The portrait advertised the skill that made her Marie Antoinette's favorite painter.

title: The Death of General Wolfe
gloss: West painted a modern British general dying at Quebec in contemporary uniform but with the pathos of a Lamentation. It scandalized academic convention and redefined history painting around current events.

title: Watson and the Shark
gloss: Copley dramatizes a real shark attack on a young sailor in Havana harbor as rescuers lunge from a rowboat. Its news-story subject and theatrical horror anticipated Romantic disaster painting.

title: Napoleon I on His Imperial Throne
wikipedia: Napoleon I on His Imperial Throne
gloss: Ingres enthrones Napoleon frontally with scepter and golden laurel, half Jupiter, half Carolingian icon. Its hieratic precision unsettled critics but became a defining image of imperial absolutism.

title: Portrait of Monsieur Bertin
gloss: Ingres's press magnate leans forward with hands clamped on his knees, embodying the confident bourgeoisie of the July Monarchy. Its unsparing realism within flawless drawing made it a landmark of portraiture.

title: The Ancient of Days
gloss: Blake's crouching deity Urizen reaches down from a fiery disc with golden compasses to circumscribe creation. The visionary frontispiece to 'Europe a Prophecy' became his most iconic single image.

title: The Abbey in the Oakwood
gloss: Monks bear a coffin through the ruined choir of a Gothic abbey among bare oaks in winter twilight. Friedrich's funereal landscape fuses German Romantic piety, death, and the sublime.

title: The Sea of Ice
gloss: Friedrich stacks shattered ice slabs into a jagged pyramid that crushes a barely visible shipwreck. The Arctic disaster scene is Romanticism's bleakest statement of nature's indifference to human ambition.

title: The Dog
gloss: A dog's head peers over a dark slope into a vast empty field of ochre, one of the Black Paintings Goya made on the walls of his house. Its desolate near-abstraction has haunted painters ever since.

title: The Death of Sardanapalus
gloss: Delacroix's Assyrian king watches impassively from his bed as his concubines, horses, and treasure are destroyed around him. Inspired by Byron, its swirling crimson violence is Romanticism at full pitch.

title: Rain, Steam and Speed - The Great Western Railway
wikipedia: Rain, Steam and Speed – The Great Western Railway
gloss: Turner dissolves a Great Western locomotive crossing Maidenhead bridge into driving rain and golden haze, a hare racing ahead of it. Modern speed swallowed whole into Romantic atmosphere.

title: The Heart of the Andes
gloss: Church's ten-foot panorama compresses Ecuador's ecology from snowcap to jungle into one luminous view. Exhibited as a single-painting spectacle with opera glasses, it crowned Hudson River School ambition.

title: The Sower
gloss: Millet monumentalizes a peasant striding down a darkening hillside, flinging seed with a fixed rhythm. Its heroic treatment of field labor unsettled Salon audiences and deeply influenced Van Gogh.

title: The Third-Class Carriage
gloss: Daumier crowds a weary grandmother, a nursing mother, and a sleeping boy into the cheap seats of a railway car. His broad, graphic handling gives modern urban poverty a quiet monumental dignity.

title: The Apotheosis of War
gloss: A pyramid of skulls under circling crows on a ruined steppe, dedicated 'to all great conquerors, past, present and to come.' Vereshchagin distilled his battlefield reportage into one antiwar emblem.

title: Madame X
gloss: Sargent's portrait of Virginie Gautreau in a plunging black gown, with haughty profile and lavender-pale skin, scandalized the 1884 Salon and drove him to London. He later called it the best thing he had done.

title: Morning in a Pine Forest
gloss: Shishkin painted the mist-filled pines and dawn light; Savitsky added the four playing bear cubs. Endlessly reproduced, famously on Russian candy wrappers, it is Russia's most familiar forest landscape.

title: The Gulf Stream
gloss: Homer strands a lone Black sailor on a dismasted sloop circled by sharks, a waterspout looming behind. Painted after his Bahamas voyages, it is his starkest image of man against the sea.

title: Automat
gloss: A lone woman in a cloche hat stares into her coffee cup in a late-night automat, the black window behind reflecting only two rows of receding lights. Hopper's quintessential image of urban solitude.

title: Beata Beatrix
gloss: Rossetti paints Dante's Beatrice in ecstatic trance as a red dove delivers a poppy, an elegy for his wife Elizabeth Siddal, who died of laudanum. A defining Pre-Raphaelite image of love and loss.

title: The Light of the World
gloss: Hunt's lantern-bearing Christ knocks at a long-shut, weed-choked door that has no outer handle: the soul must open from within. World tours and engravings made it the most famous devotional image of its age.

title: King Cophetua and the Beggar Maid
wikipedia: King Cophetua and the Beggar Maid (Burne-Jones)
King Cophetua and the Beggar Maid (painting)
gloss: Burne-Jones's armored king sits at the feet of the beggar maid he will marry, in a jewel-toned vertical dream of brass and dusk. Its medieval romance and languid idealism crown late Pre-Raphaelite art.

title: The Magpie
gloss: A lone magpie perches on a wattle gate in fresh snow that casts blue shadows. Monet's luminous winter scene, rejected by the Salon, is now one of the most loved snowscapes in art.

title: The Floor Scrapers
gloss: Three bare-backed workers plane a Parisian parquet floor in raking window light. Rejected by the Salon as 'vulgar,' Caillebotte's urban laborers became an Impressionist landmark of modern subject matter.

title: L'Absinthe
gloss: A glazed-eyed woman sits before her absinthe glass beside a rumpled companion in a cafe, cropped off-center as if glimpsed in passing. Degas's clinical slice of modern life scandalized Victorian London.

title: The Gare Saint-Lazare
gloss: Monet set his easel inside Paris's busiest rail terminus to paint locomotives dissolving into steam beneath the glass shed. The series made industrial modernity a fit subject for Impressionism.

title: Summer's Day
gloss: Two fashionable women drift in a rowboat on the Bois de Boulogne lake, rendered in Morisot's flickering zigzag strokes. A quintessential Impressionist study of leisure, light, and water.

title: The Umbrellas
gloss: Renoir began this crowded Parisian street of bobbing umbrellas in feathery Impressionist style and finished it years later in crisp, Ingres-inspired drawing; the change of manner is visible across one canvas.

title: Tiger in a Tropical Storm (Surprised!)
gloss: Rousseau's first jungle picture: a wide-eyed tiger crouches through wind-whipped, rain-slashed foliage. Mocked at the Salon des Independants, its stylized vegetation became his signature world.

title: Moulin Rouge: La Goulue
gloss: Lautrec's first poster: the dancer La Goulue kicks through the cancan between a silhouetted crowd and her lanky partner Valentin. Its flat shapes and bold cropping made the street poster modern art.

title: When Will You Marry?
gloss: Two Tahitian women crouch among flat planes of green, yellow, and orange, one wearing a white marriage flower. In 2015 it made headlines as one of the most expensive paintings ever sold.

title: Starry Night Over the Rhone
gloss: Van Gogh painted Arles's gaslit quay at night from the riverbank itself, the Great Bear overhead and lamplight streaking gold across the water. The calmer nocturne that preceded The Starry Night.

title: The Night Cafe
gloss: Van Gogh's all-night Arles cafe glows in clashing blood-red and green under sulfurous lamps. He said he tried to show a place where one could 'ruin oneself, go mad, or commit a crime.'

title: The Large Bathers
gloss: Cezanne's largest canvas locks nude bathers and arching trees into one architectural triangle. Left unresolved after seven years of work, its constructed forms opened the road to Matisse and Cubism.

title: The Dream
gloss: A nude reclines on a velvet sofa improbably set in moonlit jungle thick with lions, lotuses, and a snake charmer's music. Rousseau's last great painting crowned his self-taught visionary style.

title: Isle of the Dead
gloss: A shrouded figure is rowed toward a cypress-filled rock island under a darkening sky. Bocklin's funereal vision, painted in five versions, hung in countless homes and obsessed Freud, Dali, and Rachmaninoff.

title: Hope
gloss: Watts's blindfolded Hope sits atop the globe, straining to hear the one unbroken string of her lyre. The Symbolist allegory inspired sermons and, through one of them, Barack Obama's 'Audacity of Hope.'

title: Death and Life
gloss: Klimt sets a grinning skeleton in patterned robes against a tower of sleeping humanity wrapped in flowered quilts. The allegory won first prize at the 1911 International Art Exhibition in Rome.

title: The Cyclops
gloss: Redon's gentle one-eyed Polyphemus peers shyly over a flowering hillside at the sleeping nymph Galatea. Painted in his late color period, it turns a monster myth into a tender Symbolist dreamscape.

title: The Sick Child
gloss: Munch's dying sister Sophie rests against a pillow as her grieving aunt bows beside her, the paint scraped and scarred. He called it his breakthrough, the seed of his lifelong art of memory and loss.

title: Blue Horse I
gloss: Marc's young blue horse bows its head against rolling rainbow hills. For Der Blaue Reiter, blue stood for the spiritual; the painting remains German Expressionism's gentlest icon.

title: Fate of the Animals
gloss: Marc's forest splinters into red shards as deer and horses twist beneath falling trees; he inscribed it 'all being is flaming agony.' Painted on the eve of World War I, in which he died at Verdun.

title: Self-Portrait with Physalis
gloss: Schiele tilts his cropped head against sprigs of orange lantern fruit, his gaze at once defiant and wary. Its taut line and jagged self-scrutiny distill Viennese Expressionist portraiture.
imageFile: Egon Schiele - Self-Portrait with Physalis - Google Art Project.jpg

title: The Night
gloss: Intruders torture a family in a cramped attic in Beckmann's nightmarish allegory of postwar Germany. Its compressed space and cold brutality mark Expressionism's turn toward the New Objectivity.

title: Violin and Candlestick
gloss: Braque shatters a violin and candlestick into shifting brown-gray facets seen from every angle at once. A textbook image of Analytic Cubism, painted in close daily dialogue with Picasso.

title: Nude Descending a Staircase, No. 2
gloss: Duchamp fractures a figure into staccato machine-like planes caught mid-descent. Rejected by the Cubists and mocked at the 1913 Armory Show as 'an explosion in a shingle factory,' it made him famous.

title: Portrait of Picasso
gloss: Gris renders his Bateau-Lavoir neighbor in crystalline blue facets, palette in hand: homage from Cubism's so-called third musketeer. Its ordered geometry announces his cooler, more systematic Cubism.
imageFile: Juan Gris - Portrait of Pablo Picasso - Google Art Project.jpg

title: The City
gloss: Leger assembles billboards, scaffolding, stenciled letters, and robotic figures into a jazzy urban machine. His tubular Cubism celebrates the modern metropolis instead of dissecting still lifes.

title: Three Musicians
gloss: Picasso's masked Pierrot, Harlequin, and monk play music in flat interlocking planes like cut paper. The jaunty trio is the grand summation of Synthetic Cubism.

title: Street Light
gloss: Balla's electric streetlamp radiates chevrons of colored light that outshine the crescent moon beside it. A Futurist manifesto in paint: modern technology triumphant over romantic nature.

title: The Funeral of the Anarchist Galli
wikipedia: The Funeral of the Anarchist Galli
gloss: Carra recalls a riot he witnessed at an anarchist's funeral as a whirl of black figures, lances, and red banners under slashing light. Futurist dynamism applied to political violence.

title: The Old Guitarist
gloss: A gaunt blind musician folds around his guitar in near-monochrome blue. The masterpiece of Picasso's Blue Period, painted in poverty in Barcelona, with a ghostly earlier figure visible beneath the paint.

title: The Ten Largest, No. 7, Adulthood
gloss: One of af Klint's ten monumental canvases charting the stages of life in swirling botanical forms and looping script, painted years before Kandinsky's abstraction and kept hidden for decades at her wish.
imageFile: Hilma af Klint - The Ten Largest No. 7 - Adulthood - 1907.jpg

title: Reclining Nude
gloss: Modigliani's amber nude stretches across crimson velvet, her almond face serene. The 1917 nude series got his only solo show closed by Paris police; this canvas later sold for over $170 million.

title: I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold
wikipedia: I Saw the Figure 5 in Gold
gloss: Demuth's poster-portrait of poet William Carlos Williams recreates his poem: a gold 5 echoing through red fire-truck planes and city lights. A Precisionist landmark that anticipated Pop.

title: Cow's Skull: Red, White, and Blue
wikipedia: Cow's Skull: Red, White, and Blue
gloss: O'Keeffe centers a bleached desert skull against a red, white, and blue band, half icon, half flag. Her wry answer to calls for the Great American Painting fused Southwest bones with national myth.

title: Man at the Crossroads
gloss: Rivera's Rockefeller Center mural was chiseled off the wall in 1934 after he refused to remove Lenin; he repainted it in Mexico City as Man, Controller of the Universe. The defining scandal of Depression-era mural art.

title: Broadway Boogie Woogie
gloss: Mondrian's last completed painting pulses with small blocks of yellow, red, and blue along gridded avenues: Manhattan's lights set to boogie-woogie rhythm. His austere geometry learns to dance.

title: The Song of Love
gloss: A classical head, a surgeon's rubber glove, and a green ball meet on a melancholy arcaded square. De Chirico's Metaphysical juxtapositions moved Magritte to tears and seeded Surrealism.

title: Harlequin's Carnival
gloss: Miro fills a blue-walled room with cavorting biomorphic creatures, music, and a ladder of escape, painted, he said, from hunger-induced hallucinations. A founding image of Surrealist automatism.

title: Self-Portrait (Inn of the Dawn Horse)
gloss: Carrington perches wild-haired in a chair as a hyena mirrors her stance and a rocking horse gallops free outside the window. Her best-known canvas announces Surrealism's untamed feminine mythology.

title: The Broken Column
gloss: Kahlo splits her torso open to reveal a crumbling Ionic column in place of her spine, her flesh studded with nails. Painted after spinal surgery, it is her starkest image of pain endured.

title: Christ of Saint John of the Cross
wikipedia: Christ of Saint John of the Cross
gloss: Dali suspends Christ on a cross seen steeply from above, floating over the bay of Port Lligat without nails or blood. Based on a Spanish mystic's drawing, it is his most popular religious work.

title: The Son of Man
gloss: Magritte's bowler-hatted self-portrait, its face hidden behind a hovering green apple, plays on the visible that hides the visible. It became the universal shorthand for Surrealism.

title: Woman III
gloss: De Kooning's slashing, wide-eyed woman fuses figure and ground in aggressive strokes, one of six Women from 1951-1953. Quietly traded out of Tehran in 1994, it later sold for $137.5 million.

title: Vir Heroicus Sublimis
gloss: Newman's eighteen-foot field of cadmium red, divided by five thin vertical 'zips,' was meant to be seen from up close, engulfing the viewer. Its Latin title proposes man, heroic and sublime.

title: Mountains and Sea
gloss: Frankenthaler stained thinned paint directly into raw canvas, letting washes of blue, pink, and green bloom like watercolor. Painted at twenty-three, it launched Color Field painting.

title: Orange, Red, Yellow
gloss: Three soft-edged rectangles of saturated orange, red, and yellow hover on a vermilion field. In 2012 it set the auction record for postwar art, emblematic of Rothko's color-field sublimity.

title: Elegy to the Spanish Republic No. 110
gloss: Black ovals and slab verticals march across a white field in Motherwell's recurring funeral chant for the Spanish Civil War. He painted more than one hundred Elegies; No. 110 hangs in the Guggenheim.
imageFile: Robert Motherwell's 'Elegy to the Spanish Republic No. 110'.jpg

title: Flag
gloss: Johns rebuilt the American flag in encaustic over collaged newsprint, making a painting that is also the thing it depicts. Its deadpan object-ness broke with Abstract Expressionism and fed Pop.

title: Drowning Girl
gloss: Lifted from a romance comic panel, a tearful heroine in stylized waves declares she would 'rather sink than call Brad for help.' Benday dots and melodrama distilled into a Pop Art icon.

title: Just What Is It That Makes Today's Homes So Different, So Appealing?
wikipedia: Just what is it that makes today's homes so different, so appealing?
gloss: Hamilton furnishes a modern living room from magazine ads: a bodybuilder with a giant POP lollipop, a pin-up, a Ford logo, a tin of ham. Made for the 1956 show This Is Tomorrow, it is Pop Art's founding collage.

title: Portrait of an Artist (Pool with Two Figures)
wikipedia: Portrait of an Artist (Pool with Two Figures)
gloss: A clothed man gazes down at a swimmer gliding underwater in a sunlit pool against Provencal hills. In 2018 it briefly became the most expensive work by a living artist ever auctioned.

title: Study after Velazquez's Portrait of Pope Innocent X
wikipedia: Study after Velázquez's Portrait of Pope Innocent X
gloss: Bacon traps Velazquez's pope in a cage of vertical streaks, mouth torn open in a silent scream. The most famous of his roughly forty-five 'screaming popes.'
imageFile: Study after Velazquez's Portrait of Pope Innocent X.jpg

title: Untitled (Skull)
gloss: A grinning blue-black skull-head crackles with graffiti energy over a subway-blue field. Painted at twenty-one, it sold for $110.5 million in 2017, then the auction record for any American artist.

title: No Woman, No Cry
gloss: Ofili's weeping woman holds a tiny photograph of murdered teenager Stephen Lawrence in every tear, the canvas propped on varnished elephant dung. The Turner Prize winner's most moving meditation on grief.

title: Benefits Supervisor Sleeping
gloss: Freud's monumental nude of Sue Tilley asleep on a battered sofa renders flesh in thick, unidealized impasto. In 2008 it set the auction record for a work by a living artist.

title: Girl with Balloon
gloss: Banksy's stencil of a girl reaching for a heart-shaped balloon first appeared on a London bridge. In 2018 a framed version shredded itself at Sotheby's the moment it sold, becoming 'Love Is in the Bin.'
